bid_claims.quote_text: WDC submitted a written indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $6.60 – $7.10 per share in cash.
Company D submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price of $5.75 per share in cash, along with a draft exclusivity agreement.
Company H submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $5.00 – $5.75 per share in cash.
WDC submitted a written second-round indication of interest at a price per share of $9.15 in cash
WDC submitted a revised written indication of interest to acquire the company at a price range of $6.60 to $7.10 per share in cash.
WDC submitted a written indication of interest of $6.85 per share in cash and indicated it could be in a position to announce a transaction on June 24, 2013.

event_claims.description: Company H contacted BofA Merrill Lynch expressing interest in a potential transaction with sTec.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 1, 2013, Company H contacted representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch expressing an interest in a potential transaction with sTec.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch sent a non-disclosure agreement to Company H.
event_claims.quote_text: on May 2, 2013, at the direction of our chairman on behalf of the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch sent a non-disclosure agreement to Company H.

event_claims.description: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company H.
event_claims.quote_text: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company H.

event_claims.description: Company G indicated it would not continue in the process.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on May 3, 2013, Company G indicated it would not continue in the process.

event_claims.description: WDC submitted a written indication of interest to acquire the company.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 3, 2013, WDC submitted a written indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $6.60 – $7.10 per share in cash.

event_claims.description: Company D submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company.
event_claims.quote_text: Company D submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price of $5.75 per share in cash, along with a draft exclusivity agreement.

event_claims.description: Company H submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company.
event_claims.quote_text: Company H submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $5.00 – $5.75 per share in cash.

event_claims.description: Company H was told its price range was not sufficient to move forward in the process.
event_claims.quote_text: representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch contacted Company H and indicated that the price range Company H had submitted was not sufficient to move them forward in the process.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D.
event_claims.quote_text: After the meeting, at the direction of the board, BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D, requesting a response by May 28, 2013.

event_claims.description: WDC submitted a written second-round indication of interest at $9.15 per share in cash.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 28, 2013, WDC submitted a written second-round indication of interest at a price per share of $9.15 in cash

event_claims.description: Company D needed more time to continue due diligence and submit a written indication of interest and merger agreement mark-up.
event_claims.quote_text: Company D needed approximately two weeks of additional time to continue its ongoing due diligence review and to submit a written indication of interest and mark-up of the merger agreement.

event_claims.description: WDC verbally indicated that its $9.15 per share indication was its best possible price.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 30, 2013, representatives of WDC verbally indicated that WDC’s indication of interest to acquire the company at a price of $9.15 per share was WDC’s best possible price

event_claims.description: The board determined to move forward with WDC on its proposed terms.
event_claims.quote_text: Our board of directors unanimously determined to move forward with WDC on their proposed terms, including endeavoring to complete diligence and negotiation of the merger agreement and ancillary agreements to be in a position to announce a transaction on June 3, 2013.

event_claims.description: WDC communicated it was reevaluating its interest and was not prepared to move forward at that time.
event_claims.quote_text: WDC was reevaluating its interest in acquiring sTec, and that WDC was not prepared to move forward with a transaction with sTec at that time.

event_claims.description: Company D indicated it was disengaging from the process.
event_claims.quote_text: Company D would not be in a position to actively conduct due diligence for more than two weeks and was disengaging from the process.

event_claims.description: WDC submitted a revised written indication of interest with a price range of $6.60 to $7.10 per share in cash.
event_claims.quote_text: On June 10, 2013, WDC submitted a revised written indication of interest to acquire the company at a price range of $6.60 to $7.10 per share in cash.

event_claims.description: WDC submitted a written indication of interest at $6.85 per share in cash.
event_claims.quote_text: On June 14, 2013, WDC submitted a written indication of interest of $6.85 per share in cash and indicated it could be in a position to announce a transaction on June 24, 2013.

event_claims.description: The special committee determined to move forward with WDC on the terms in WDC's June 14 indication of interest, subject to final terms.
event_claims.quote_text: the special committee unanimously determined that they believed that it was in the best interests of sTec and its shareholders to move forward with a transaction with WDC on the terms set forth in WDC’s indication of interest received on June 14, 2013

event_claims.description: The board instructed BofA Merrill Lynch to communicate authorization to move forward with WDC.
event_claims.quote_text: instructed BofA Merrill Lynch to communicate the board’s authorization to move forward to WDC.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch informed WDC that the board authorized moving forward with a transaction with WDC at $6.85 per share, subject to final terms.
event_claims.quote_text: representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch informed WDC that our board of directors had authorized moving forward with a transaction with WDC on the terms set forth in WDC’s proposal received June 14, 2013, at a price of $6.85 per share, subject to negotiating final terms of the merger agreement and ancillary agreements relating to such transaction.

participation_count_claims.quote_text:
On May 3, 2013, WDC submitted a written indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $6.60 – $7.10 per share in cash. Also on May 3, 2013, Company G indicated it would not continue in the process.

Thereafter, on May 3, 2013, at the direction of Dr. Daly, BofA Merrill Lynch provided the special committee with an update of the status of discussions with potential acquirers.

On May 6, 2013, our board of directors held a telephonic special meeting, attended only by the independent directors. The independent directors discussed, among other matters, the status of the strategic review process.

On May 8, 2013, sTec announced its results for the first quarter of fiscal year 2013.

Also on May 8, 2013, sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company H.

Also on May 8, 2013, the special committee held a telephonic meeting. Messrs. Cook, Saman and Morales and representatives from Gibson Dunn were in attendance. The special committee discussed in detail sTec’s standalone financial projections prepared by management and the further review that needed to be undertaken with management on management’s financial model. Gibson Dunn reviewed certain considerations related to the special committee’s fiduciary duties in connection with the strategic review process. Representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch joined for a portion of the meeting and reported on BofA Merrill Lynch’s interactions to date with potential acquirers and the special committee discussed with the representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch the preliminary proposal received from WDC and a number of financial matters relating to the proposal.

On May 10, 2013, sTec held a management presentation with Company H.

Also on May 10, 2013, sTec granted access to WDC to the virtual data room.

Also on May 10, 2013, Company D submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price of $5.75 per share in cash, along with a draft exclusivity agreement.

On May 14, 2013, sTec granted access to Company D to the virtual data room.

On May 15, 2013, Company H submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $5.00 – $5.75 per share in cash.
BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D, requesting a response by May 28, 2013.
